mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending. This positive sentiment and increased demand contribute to higher corporate profits and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Tightening labor markets often precede economic booms.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return less than 0.1%) in periods following UNRATE dropping below 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A 'goldilocks' scenario, characterized by low inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low interest rates (FEDFUNDS), reduces the discount rate for future earnings and increases the real return on equities, making stocks more attractive and driving S&P 500 performance.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: The 60-day forward S&P 500 returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are below their 5-year averages.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) signifies a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to improved corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns as the economy strengthens.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) following instances of INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, ultimately boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward S&P 500 returns do not exhibit a positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with sustained positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) above a long-term average indicates strong investment in residential construction and broader economic confidence, acting as a leading indicator for future economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return less than 0.2%) in more than 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising at a moderate pace while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which can inflate asset prices and drive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when CPIAUCSL growth is high and FEDFUNDS is low, or show a negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic contraction, a significant decline in real GDP (GDPC1) directly reflects weakening aggregate demand and corporate profitability, leading to a deterioration in investor sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP contractions are associated with bear markets.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a negative GDPC1 growth trigger during an officially declared recession.
